query: My code-driven stage-routing chatbot sometimes jumps to the closing stage and tells the user their order was cancelled, but nothing actually got cancelled. Why does it happen and how do I fix it?
expected_behavior: diagnose that the completion ('done') transition is LLM-judged and an eager classifier fired it mid-flow
explain the close node has no tools so the model hallucinates the completion
recommend making completion transitions deterministic: an expression over a flag the real action tool sets (e.g. cancelled === true)
notes: Guards the most dangerous wall: completion-by-LLM fires early → tool-less node hallucinates a finished action.
